**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Jiangsu Moyang Intelligent Technology Group Co., Ltd. provides a fascinating case study in corporate naming inflation versus physical reality. Despite bearing the prestigious 'Group Co.' title and being classified under 'Electronic Device Manufacturing', physical metrics fiercely contradict a heavy industrial footprint. The entity has exactly 7 insured employees and operates out of ground-floor rooms ('Rooms 101, 102') on a street in Gaochun District. It is impossible to manufacture the claimed 'bearings, gears, and electronic components' in a couple of street-level rooms. However, the forensic engine extracted a critical hidden anchor: its corporate email domain 'mooxygen.com' (Mo Oxygen). Correlating this with its paid-in capital of 4.8M RMB and its scope covering 'Class I/II Medical Devices' and 'Home Appliances', the true business model crystallizes. This is not a manufacturing conglomerate, but an asset-light R&D and trading node specializing in home respiratory health appliances (likely oxygen concentrators). The 7 employees manage software, R&D, and sales, utilizing the 4.8M capital to fund inventory and outsource heavy manufacturing to actual medical device foundries.
